title: Advisor KPI Rollup to Wilbur — executive accountability layer for the advisor fleet
what: After each advisor expedition, the advisor writes a wilbur_kpi_report.json to its own folder. Wilbur reads these on its nightly scan and produces a fleet-wide advisor health dashboard. Advisors must satisfy Wilbur's KPI expectations before their budget allocation is renewed in the next init cycle. Wilbur is the executive — advisors report to it, not to Ozi.
why: Without a top-down accountability layer, advisor quality degrades silently. Vera's coverage_score can drop to 0.2 and nothing notices. Wilbur provides the executive demand signal: 'This advisor is under-performing — either fix it or Ozi stops allocating budget to it.'

kpis.p1_dimensions_at_target: bool — all P1 dimensions at >= 0.75 coverage
kpis.reflections_current: bool — reflection_latest.json is newer than expedition_log

fields.notes: string — free-form advisor commentary for Wilbur

fleet_health_dashboard.what: Wilbur aggregates all spoke advisor KPI reports into hub/WAI-Hub/advisor-fleet-health.json
fleet_health_dashboard.surfaced_in: Wilbur nightly scan output → WAI wakeup brief (fleet health section)

escalation_path.yellow_one_miss: Wilbur flags in fleet health dashboard — no action required
escalation_path.orange_two_consecutive: Wilbur surfaces in wakeup brief with recommendation
escalation_path.red_three_consecutive: Wilbur recommends Ozi reduce budget allocation by 50%
escalation_path.black_five_consecutive: Wilbur recommends advisor retirement — routes notice to framework

relationship_to_ozi: Ozi dispatches advisors (operational). Wilbur evaluates them (executive). Ozi sees schedule; Wilbur sees outcomes. Neither overrules the other — Wilbur's recommendation routes to Mario's attention queue.
relationship_to_pathgraph: Wilbur correlates advisor findings with PathGraph — a finding that maps to a decision the PathGraph records as intentional is deprioritized. A finding in a module with no PathGraph coverage is elevated.
